title: The Day a Shower Was Everything

story.opening: <break time="400ms"/>There was a day - <break time="350ms"/>deep in a flare - <break time="300ms"/>when I took a shower. <break time="250ms"/>Just a shower.
story.body: <break time="350ms"/>And I cried. <break time="300ms"/>Not from sadness. <break time="250ms"/>From... <break time="200ms"/>I don't know. <break time="300ms"/>Pride? <break time="250ms"/>Relief? <break time="400ms"/>I'd been horizontal for four days. <break time="350ms"/>Everything hurt. <break time="300ms"/>And I managed to stand under water for five minutes. <break time="400ms"/>A friend texted asking what I did that day. <break time="350ms"/>I almost lied. <break time="300ms"/>Said 'nothing.' <break time="250ms"/>Because a shower felt pathetic to admit.
story.reflection: <break time="400ms"/>But it wasn't nothing. <break time="350ms"/>When your body is fighting you, <break time="300ms"/>everything costs more. <break time="250ms"/>Those small things? <break time="200ms"/>They're not small. <break time="300ms"/>They're victories.

when_to_share: When someone feels ashamed of only managing 'basic' tasks
